global.title: Bee Reactor Adapter Package

coreIdeas.contextPhrase: Top-level monorepo shape: pnpm, two packages — the entry point for the package structure.
What the two npm packages are: adapter + connect fork.
Adapter source layout: src/channel, src/swarm-client, src/chat, src/plugin — the file-level code map.
src/channel details — where all Swarm-sync code lives.
Infrastructure modules wrap Bee APIs, wallet crypto, stamps — operational plumbing.
src/chat and src/plugin host the PSS chat runtime and plugin orchestration.
src/plugin/init.ts — how the adapter exposes runtime status to Connect settings.
Why the package exists: enables serverless Powerhouse Connect on Swarm.
Operational preconditions the package assumes (full Bee, mutable stamp, wallet, P2P).

global.description: Structure of the bee-reactor-adaptor monorepo: two npm packages, the src/channel + swarm-client + chat + plugin layout, and operational requirements.
global.orientation: The bee-reactor-adaptor is the concrete code artifact that turns Path A into a real install. It's a pnpm monorepo with two published packages: the adapter itself and a Connect fork. Inside the adapter, src/channel/ holds the Swarm-sync code, src/swarm-client.ts wraps Bee APIs, and src/chat and src/plugin house the PSS chat runtime and plugin orchestration. This MOC collects the code-map notes, the required-environment notes, and the shared encryption/ACT dependencies that define how the package fits into a Connect install.
